metadata.title: The Ghost Job You Can't See: Five Signals That Read the Posting, Not the Prose
metadata.color_semantics: TEAL=#1F6F5C=live posting/behavioral signals/real opening/active search, CRIMSON=#BF3339=ghost posting/frozen/template/stale, GOLD=#F5D061=fill highlight only never text
metadata.note: Exclusions: no ATS vendor comparisons; no detail on how the ATS detection script works; no coverage of false negatives beyond a single spoken sentence; no equations
_variant_todo: rewrite every beat narration_text in the Teardown register (voices/teardown/VOICE.md + CLAUDE-BRAND.md) — voice only, facts unchanged
signature tangent 0-1 per video, ONLY on a clear opportunity (see SKILL)
swap the outro to the Claude outro from AUTHOR.MD :: NikBearBrown
then build audience-namespaced (audio in the new voice, scenes in the new palette)

beats.name: Title
beats.narration_text: Two job postings. Same employer. Same title. Same sponsorship tier. One of them has never been a real opening.

beats.name: ColdOpenDesk
beats.narration_text: You spend an afternoon writing a cover letter. Researching the team. Tailoring your resume. The door was never going to open.

props.headline: You spend an afternoon writing a cover letter. Researching the team.…

beats.scene_description: person at desk writing a cover letter, stack of application papers, editorial newsprint collage
beats.image_prompt: B02, person at a wooden desk writing by hand on paper, stack of job application documents, warm editorial light, newsprint collage aesthetic, desaturated tones, flat print reproduction

beats.name: TheQuestion
beats.narration_text: Reading a job posting should tell you whether it's real. Here is the case where the most compelling-sounding posting is the ghost. Why?

beats.name: GhostRate
beats.narration_text: Between 28 and 42 percent of job postings are ghosts — listings no company has active intention of filling. That figure has held steady for five years. It is structural.

beats.name: WhyGhosts
beats.narration_text: A ghost is not a cancelled role. It is a posting never connected to a hiring intention — a pipeline hedge, an investor signal, bureaucratic inertia. The description reads perfectly real.

beats.name: SpamParallel
beats.narration_text: Ghost detection is structurally identical to spam filtering. Not metaphorically — mechanically. Your spam filter does not read the message. It scores behavioral fingerprints.

beats.name: ThreeFingerprints
beats.narration_text: Three behavioral fingerprints: temporal anomaly — the posting is frozen in time. Interaction void — no portal activity. Textual homogeneity — the description is a copy.

beats.name: FiveSignals
beats.narration_text: Five signals, in order: posting age, last updated, sibling listing activity, description specificity, active search context. Together they classify behavior — not prose.

beats.name: Classifier
beats.narration_text: No single signal is decisive. Together, the way no individual word makes an email spam but the pattern does, they yield a call: live, ghost, or investigate.

beats.name: TheImplication
beats.narration_text: The compelling description is the costume. The metadata is the evidence. One false negative exists: fresh, specific-sounding harvest postings designed to beat the detector.

beats.name: HelixBio
beats.narration_text: Helix Bio, two Senior Data Scientist roles. Posting A: 9 days, refreshed twice, names a model-retraining project. Posting B: 11 weeks, frozen, description word-for-word from 18 months ago. Same employer. A is live. B is a ghost. Illustrative numbers — same mechanism.

beats.name: Endcard
beats.narration_text: The posting looked real. The signals said ghost. Don't write a cover letter for a door that was never going to open.

beats.name: OutroSeries
beats.narration_text: Part of the The Reallocation Engine series.
